channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Most people who need glasses don't have them. Can the post office change that?
item.description: In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?
item.content:encoded:
In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?

(Image credit: Subhamoy Bhattacharjee for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Cyclospora outbreaks grow in the Midwest
item.description: Michigan reports 1,250 cases of cyclospora and CDC surveillance shows cases in 17 states. The CDC has initiated a traceback investigation, as the source is not yet known.
item.content:encoded: Michigan reports 1,250 cases of cyclospora and CDC surveillance shows cases in 17 states. The CDC has initiated a traceback investigation, as the source is not yet known.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Taliban declares war on smartphones
item.description: A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.
item.content:encoded:
A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.

(Image credit: Wakil Kohsar/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Faizi

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
item.description: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.
item.content:encoded:
New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In private call, Education Dept. tried, but failed, to reassure disability advocates
item.description: The disability community has long worried about what would happen if special education oversight moved from the Education Department to another agency. Now, those moves are becoming more real.
item.content:encoded:
The disability community has long worried about what would happen if special education oversight moved from the Education Department to another agency. Now, those moves are becoming more real.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: EPA proposes weakening heavy-duty truck pollution rules
item.description: The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: 'Solo agers' are a growing group. Changes that would help them could help everyone
item.description: Many older adults navigate aging on their own — without children or a spouse. An expert says this is "a reality to be supported" rather than a crisis to be solved.
item.content:encoded:
Many older adults navigate aging on their own — without children or a spouse. An expert says this is "a reality to be supported" rather than a crisis to be solved.

(Image credit: Ashley Milne-Tyte for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Milne-Tyte

item.title: A new proposal for organ donation sparks concern
item.description: Should patients who choose euthanasia be able to die by having their vital organs removed for donation? The ethical concerns are substantial.
item.content:encoded: Should patients who choose euthanasia be able to die by having their vital organs removed for donation? The ethical concerns are substantial.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: A spirit of resilience helps Venezuelans face healthcare challenges after the quakes
item.description: The economic collapse of Venezuela has pushed its healthcare system to the brink. The disaster is now adding to the stress.
item.content:encoded:
The economic collapse of Venezuela has pushed its healthcare system to the brink. The disaster is now adding to the stress.

(Image credit: Family photo)
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Trump administration abruptly cancels grants for teen pregnancy prevention
item.description: Health departments, universities and nonprofit grantees had spent months adapting to President Trump's executive orders. They say the funding cut was completely unexpected.
item.content:encoded:
Health departments, universities and nonprofit grantees had spent months adapting to President Trump's executive orders. They say the funding cut was completely unexpected.

(Image credit: Hózhǫ́ Horizons)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin
